summary: Decompiled and improved several memory-card/snapshot routines in lbcardnew, with typed snapshot/card-entry plumbing propagated into lbsnap and baselib card descriptor APIs. The decomp.dev bot reported 4 new full matches in main/melee/lb/lbcardnew: lb_8001C4A8, lb_8001A594, lb_8001BA44, and lb_8001BC18, plus a large unmatched-item improvement for lb_8001B14C from 1.04% to 63.23%. Most of the substantive work replaces opaque UNK fields with named snapshot/free-space fields, implements snapshot scanning/sorting behavior, and refactors repeated card-entry setup logic into an inline helper.
what_changed: Implemented lb_8001B14C instead of leaving it as a stub/commented UnclePunch-map placeholder. The new code calls DVDGetCurrentDiskID, iterates CARDGetStatus over file numbers 0..0x7E, filters files by company/game code and digit-leading filename, parses the filename with strtoul, stores time/file_no/block count in SnapshotNode records, inserts them into a time-sorted linked list, copies them into lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry output entries, terminates remaining entries with file_no = -1, and frees the temporary node array.

Added struct lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry in src/melee/lb/types.h with fields time, file_no, and blocks, plus STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry) == 0x8). Added the forward typedef to src/melee/lb/forward.h and used it in lbcardnew.h.
Renamed lb_80432A68 fields at offsets 0x20, 0x24, and 0x28 from unk_20/unk_24/unk_28 to snapshot_entries/free_blocks/free_files and updated initialization and lb_8001BFD8 assignment sites.

Changed lb_8001BFD8 signature from generic UNK_T snapshot arguments to int lb_8001BFD8(int chan, lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry* snapshot_entries, int* free_blocks, int* free_files), matching the now-named internal fields.

Introduced local lbcardnew.c helper structs CardEntry and SnapshotNode. CardEntry names the repeated triple as file_size, file_flags, and data_size; SnapshotNode names next/time/file_no/blocks for snapshot scan ordering.
Refactored repeated hsd_803B2ADC/hsd_803AC3E0 card-entry setup loops into inline setup_card_entries(ctx, icon, entry), reused by lb_8001A594 and conceptually aligned with lb_8001C4A8.

Completed matching rewrites for lb_8001BA44 and lb_8001BC18 by expanding previously inline-wrapper calls into explicit task setup sequences, including lb_80019EF0, setup_task calls, lb_8001A4CC_dontinline, strncpy/memcpy into task x10, write-buffer fields, and lb_80019CB0 polling behavior.
Updated lb_8001ACEC and lb_8001AE38 to use struct CardEntry* and entries[i].data_size instead of anonymous unk0/unk4/unk8 fields.

Renamed lbsnap static struct fields: unk0 to card_result, unk8 to free_blocks, unkC to free_files, and unk10 to entries. Replaced a local private snapshot-entry struct with lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry entries[0x7F], keeping STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(struct Unk80433380_48) == 0x408).

Updated lbsnap logic to call lb_8001BFD8(chan, ptr->entries, &ptr->free_blocks, &ptr->free_files), to count entries until file_no == -1, and to reference entries[index].time/blocks/file_no instead of unk fields.

Corrected baselib hsd_803AC3E0 parameter names/order from file_offset/file_flags to file_flags/data_size and updated assignments x28[file_idx] = file_flags and x70[file_idx] = data_size in both .c and .h.

Made several formatting-only or match-supporting cleanups in lb_01F8.c, lbmthp.c, lbmthp.h, mndatadel.c, and mndatadel.static.h, mostly line wrapping, include ordering, casts, and compact initializer formatting.

Reordered/expanded compile_flags.txt warning suppressions, adding -Wno-format and moving several -Wno-* entries into sorted/earlier positions; evidence does not show a review discussion explaining this change.
smart_moves: Typed the snapshot API end-to-end instead of only naming local variables: lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry is forward-declared, included through lbcardnew.h, stored in lb_80432A68_t, and reused by lbsnap.static.h. This reduced duplicated anonymous structs and made the lbcardnew/lbsnap boundary clearer.

Preserved size/layout evidence while renaming fields by adding/keeping STATIC_ASSERTs for lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry and Unk80433380_48. This is a safe way to improve readability without silently changing ABI/layout assumptions.
Converted a repeated card-entry setup loop into an inline helper, which both removed duplicate code and likely helped lb_8001A594 match by controlling inlining and local-variable shape.

Used semantically meaningful names derived from observed usage: card_result for status-return storage, free_blocks/free_files for output counters, entries for snapshot arrays, and file_size/file_flags/data_size for card descriptor triples.

For lb_8001B14C, modeled the original behavior with a temporary linked-list insertion sort over snapshot times. The insertion condition ((*scan)->time > node->time) preserves descending time order before copying to the output array.
Handled optional output pointers in the snapshot scan path: free_blocks and free_files are checked for NULL before writing, matching a defensive API pattern useful in memory-card code.
Kept sentinel behavior explicit by filling remaining snapshot_entries[i].file_no = -1 after copied results, which lbsnap then uses to determine num entries.
Adjusted baselib hsd_803AC3E0 names and assignments to match actual caller semantics, improving cross-module type recovery rather than papering over lbcardnew callsites with misleading unk fields.
decomp_lessons: When an anonymous three-int descriptor appears across multiple memory-card functions, look for a shared semantic struct and rename all users together; here file_size/file_flags/data_size made lb_8001A594, lb_8001ACEC, lb_8001AE38, and lb_8001C4A8 easier to reason about.

For decompiling task-driven wrappers, expanding a guessed inline call into the exact setup_task sequence can close the last few bytes of a near-match, as shown by lb_8001BA44 and lb_8001BC18 moving to 100%.

Snapshot list APIs often use fixed maximum counts and sentinel entries instead of returning a count directly. In this PR, output entries are valid until file_no == -1, while free_blocks/free_files are side-channel output pointers.
Avoid leaving private duplicate structs in static headers once a real cross-module type is known. Moving the 0x8 snapshot entry into lb/types.h let lbsnap and lbcardnew share one definition.
Renaming fields in global context structs should be accompanied by all initialization and callsite updates in the same PR so the semantic meaning remains verifiable.
When matching MSL/string numeric parsing behavior, use the project-specific include path seen here, <MSL/strtoul.h>, rather than assuming a standard-library include is already available.

Baselib API parameter names matter for future callers. hsd_803AC3E0's fourth and fifth arguments are better documented as file_flags and data_size based on stores into x28 and x70 plus lbcardnew CardEntry usage.
Some changed files are formatting/match-support churn rather than new decomp logic; future searches should distinguish substantive lbcardnew/lbsnap changes from mndatadel/lbmthp formatting noise.
naming_conventions: New cross-module lb types use the lbCardNew_ prefix plus semantic noun: lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry.
Snapshot-entry fields were named time, file_no, and blocks, matching observed filename timestamp parsing, CARD file number, and stat.length >> 13 block conversion.
Global context fields at fixed offsets were renamed from unk_N to role names when usage was clear: snapshot_entries, free_blocks, free_files.
lbsnap per-channel state fields were renamed to card_result, num, free_blocks, free_files, and entries, replacing unk0/unk8/unkC/unk10.
Card-entry triple names use file_size, file_flags, and data_size, and the helper name setup_card_entries describes the HSD card descriptor setup operation.

Function names remained address-based for unknown lbcardnew routines, e.g. lb_8001B14C and lb_8001BFD8; the PR did not rename them to descriptive public names despite a removed comment noting UnclePunch called lb_8001B14C MemoryCard_ScanForSnapshots.
HSD API cleanup renamed hsd_803AC3E0 parameters to file_desc, file_idx, file_size, file_flags, and data_size.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used an inline helper setup_card_entries rather than an out-of-line function for repeated descriptor setup, likely preserving original inlining while reducing source duplication.
Added explicit stack padding arrays in lb_8001BA44 and lb_8001BC18 (u8 _[0x10] and u8 _[0x18]) to shape stack layout for matching.
Introduced a local new_var = 0x20 in lb_8001BC18 before memcpy, a common decomp tactic to influence register allocation or instruction selection.

Kept volatile locals in lb_8001ACEC/lb_8001AE38 around cached_flag/cached_data/pad_stack, suggesting stack/register-shaping from prior matching work was preserved while only the struct field names changed.
Rewrote wrapper-style calls into explicit setup_task/lb_80019CB0 sequences for lb_8001BA44 and lb_8001BC18 to reach 100% matches.
Used specific casts and local aliases in lb_8001C4A8, such as void* icon = icon_data and s32* ctx = &lb_80432A68.unk_A8, to match call signatures/layout while improving readability.
The decomp.dev bot reported +1428 matched bytes overall, with lb_8001C4A8, lb_8001A594, lb_8001BA44, and lb_8001BC18 becoming full matches and lb_8001B14C improving by +761 bytes to 63.23%.
Formatting and cast reshaping in unrelated files such as mndatadel.c and lb_01F8.c appears to be match/style-supporting; no review evidence explains whether it was required for matching.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.

The only issue comment was from decomp-dev[bot], reporting matched-code impact: 66.59% total matched code (+0.04%, +1428 bytes), 4 new lbcardnew matches, and lb_8001B14C improving from 1.04% to 63.23%.

No explicit reviewer rationale was available for compile_flags.txt warning reordering/addition, the formatting churn in mndatadel/lbmthp/lb_01F8, or remaining TODO comments such as CardEntry seeming fake.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish lb_8001B14C from 63.23% to 100% and compare whether the SnapshotNode insertion-sort structure survived.
Search for uses of lbCardNew_SnapshotEntry after PR 2483 to see whether more lbcardnew/lbsnap APIs adopted the type.
Audit hsd_803AC3E0 callers to confirm all use the corrected file_size, file_flags, data_size ordering.
Look for later renames of lb_8001B14C or lb_8001BFD8 to descriptive MemoryCard/Snapshot names once confidence improves.
Investigate whether compile_flags.txt -Wno-format addition was required by this PR or just repository-wide flag sorting/noise.
